prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident has been detected involving the 'svc_etl' service account from host 10.20.3.15. Approximately 1.1GB of data, including sensitive FINANCE_PII, was uploaded to a suspicious external domain, 'upload.evil-cdn.net' (185.220.101.44), via a curl process. Multiple security systems flagged this activity as a high-volume outbound anomaly and a data exfiltration pattern, indicating a potential compromise and significant data loss.

timeline.event: Outbound anomaly detected: 10.20.3.15 sent 1.1GB to 185.220.101.44 in 77 seconds.
Sensitive label FINANCE_PII detected in outbound transfer by 'svc_etl'.
Data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to rare external domain detected.
MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel suspected for host 10.20.3.15.
Volume spike (14x baseline) detected from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
CRITICAL alert: data_exfiltration incident involving user 'svc_etl' and destination 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data exfiltration or lateral movement.
Disable or reset credentials for the 'svc_etl' account and revoke all active sessions. Investigate the account for signs of compromise.
Block the malicious destination 'upload.evil-cdn.net' (185.220.101.44) at the perimeter firewall and proxy.
Analyze the '/data/finance_q2.zip' file and the 'curl' process on host 10.20.3.15 to determine the full scope of compromise and initial access vector.
Notify legal and privacy teams due to the confirmed exfiltration of sensitive FINANCE_PII data.
